items.title: FEMA Delays Under Kristi Noem Angered Republicans
items.description: Long delays in FEMA assistance brought bipartisan complaints, especially in North Carolina after Hurricane Helene in 2024.

items.title: How $800 Monthly Car Payments Are Hurting Car Sales
items.description: Cars have become so expensive that many Americans are putting off or not buying new cars, hurting the auto industry.

items.title: After Apalachee High School Shooting, a Hunger for Accountability
items.description: The country is casting an ever wider net in its search for accountability after mass shootings. Charging parents is an emerging tactic.

items.title: Politicians Are Trying to Control the News
items.description: The rising global attacks on the free press reveal increasingly desperate regimes attempting to snuff out attempts to find the truth.

items.title: Nature Report, Killed by Trump, Is Released Independently
items.description: A draft assessment of the health of nature in the United States is grim but shot through with bright spots and possibility.
